author: Fizzed
text:
Warm bl remaster --detach: transplant state onto .balls/worktree

The warm detach path (remaster_detach::detach) re-rooted the balls/tasks
orphan inside store.state_worktree_dir(), which under master_url resolves
to .balls/state-repo, then cleared master_url. Every subsequent
Store::discover re-resolves the layout fresh: with master_url gone,
state_worktree_for returns .balls/worktree — a path the detach never
created or migrated to. Result: a standalone-then-flipped repo silently
read a stale .balls/worktree (federated tasks invisible), and a
seeded-federated repo hard-failed discovery on the missing worktree.

Fix: after re-rooting, when the state checkout is the balls-owned
.balls/state-repo clone, transplant the detached branch onto the project
git's .balls/worktree — the layout discover resolves once master_url is
cleared. adopt_state_into_worktree tears down any stale pre-flip
worktree, fetches the re-rooted balls/tasks into the project git, and
checks it out at .balls/worktree. The legacy state_remote detach path
(state worktree already .balls/worktree) is unchanged.

Detach is also lifted into its own module (remaster_detach.rs) so both
it and the remaster reconcile core stay under the line cap; the public
path balls::remaster::{detach, try_cold_detach} is preserved by re-export.

Tests: warm detach of (a) a standalone-then-flipped repo and (b) a
seeded-federated repo — bl list/bl create after detach see the detached
task set, no error, no stale branch.
